domain: stained glass leaded panel construction (Gothic cathedral; each colored glass piece bounded by H-channel lead cames + soldered joints; full-size cartoon design as offline plan; closed-mesh structural integrity)
specific_mechanism: A stained glass panel is assembled by laying colored glass pieces into H-section lead cames that hold each piece and separate it from neighbors. Cames are soldered at every junction forming a closed mesh. The design starts as a CARTOON — a full-size drawing on a plaster table with each piece numbered. Closed-mesh structural integrity is required: any unsealed gap = piece falls out.
llm_application: Cames-bounded concept-compartment LLM activations: partition the residual-stream dimension axis offline into N concept compartments via a learned block-diagonal orthogonal projection (the came-network). Each compartment is bounded by a low-rank projection (lead came) that strictly separates its signal from neighbors. A CARTOON — human-authored routing graph specifying which compartment activates for which prompt-class — runs at inference. A 'soldering loss' adversarially minimises cross-compartment cosine during training. Properties: (1) every neuron has a known concept-name (interpretability); (2) compartment knockout (intervention) is precise; (3) closed-mesh integrity audit at each layer flags any cross-compartment leak as a 'broken came' violation.
novelty_claim: Concept Layers (2502.13632) provide named intervention handles but not compartment-bounded came-mesh structure. SAE feature decomposition gives sparse features but does not enforce closed-mesh integrity. Mechanistic interpretability literature finds switch-features but does not impose offline-authored cartoon routing. The closed-mesh + adversarial soldering-loss + human-authored cartoon combination is not in surveyed prior art.
